title: The steam car
subtitle: The successes and failures of the Doble brothers and their steam cars

phrases.def: E型车（多布尔蒸汽车的成熟型号）

sentences.en: When primitive automobiles first began to appear in the 1800s, their engines were based on steam power.
sentences.zh: 19世纪早期原始汽车最初出现时，其发动机是以蒸汽为动力的。

grammar.type: when 时间状语从句 + 被动语态
grammar.note: When primitive automobiles first began to appear in the 1800s 为时间状语从句；主句 their engines were based on steam power，be based on 表“以……为基础”。

sentences.en: Steam had already enjoyed a long and successful career in the railways, so it was only natural that the technology evolved into a miniaturized version which was separate from the trains.
sentences.zh: 蒸汽在铁路领域已经有过漫长而成功的历程，因此这项技术演变成一种脱离火车的微缩版本，也就再自然不过了。

grammar.type: 过去完成时 + so 结果 + it 形式主语 + 定语从句
grammar.note: 前半 Steam had already enjoyed a long and successful career，为过去完成时；so it was only natural that...，it 为形式主语，that the technology evolved into a miniaturized version 为主语从句；which was separate from the trains 为定语从句修饰 version。

sentences.en: But these early cars inherited steam’s weaknesses along with its strengths.
sentences.zh: 但这些早期汽车在继承蒸汽优点的同时，也承袭了它的弱点。

grammar.type: 主谓宾 + along with 状语
grammar.note: 主干是 these early cars inherited steam’s weaknesses；along with its strengths 为介词短语作伴随状语，表“连同、以及”。

sentences.en: The boilers had to be lit by hand, and they required about twenty minutes to build up pressure before they could be driven.
sentences.zh: 锅炉必须用手点燃，而且在能够驱动汽车之前，需要大约二十分钟来积聚压力。

grammar.type: 被动语态 + 并列句 + before 时间状语从句
grammar.note: 前句 The boilers had to be lit by hand 为被动语态；后句 they required about twenty minutes to build up pressure，before they could be driven 为时间状语从句。

sentences.en: Furthermore, their water reservoirs only lasted for about thirty miles before needing replenishment.
sentences.zh: 此外，它们的储水箱大约只能维持三十英里，之后就需要补充。

grammar.type: 主谓 + before 状语
grammar.note: 主干是 their water reservoirs only lasted for about thirty miles；before needing replenishment 为介词短语作时间状语；Furthermore 表递进。

sentences.en: Despite such shortcomings, these newly designed self-propelled carriages offered quick transportation, and by the early 1900s it was not uncommon to see such machines shuttling wealthy citizens around town.
sentences.zh: 尽管存在这些缺点，这些新设计的自走式车辆还是提供了快捷的交通方式，到20世纪初，看到这类机器载着富裕市民在城里往返已不算罕见。

grammar.type: 让步状语 + 并列句 + it 形式主语
grammar.note: Despite such shortcomings 为让步状语；前句 these newly designed self-propelled carriages offered quick transportation；后句 it was not uncommon to see such machines shuttling wealthy citizens，it 为形式主语，see sth doing 为复合宾语。

sentences.en: But the glory days of steam cars were few.
sentences.zh: 但蒸汽汽车的辉煌岁月十分短暂。

grammar.type: 主系表
grammar.note: 主干是 the glory days of steam cars were few，few 作表语表“为数不多”；But 表转折。

sentences.en: A new technology called the Internal Combustion Engine soon appeared, which offered the ability to drive down the road just moments after starting up.
sentences.zh: 一种名为内燃机的新技术很快出现了，它使汽车在启动后片刻即可上路行驶。

grammar.type: 过去分词定语 + 非限定性定语从句
grammar.note: 主干 A new technology... soon appeared；called the Internal Combustion Engine 为过去分词短语修饰 technology；which offered the ability to drive down the road 为非限定性定语从句，just moments after starting up 为时间状语。

sentences.en: At first, these noisy gasoline cars were unpopular because they were more complicated to operate and they had difficult hand-crank starters, which were known to break arms when the engines backfired.
sentences.zh: 起初，这些嘈杂的汽油汽车并不受欢迎，因为它们操作起来更复杂，而且配有难用的手摇启动器——众所周知，当发动机回火时，这种启动器会打断人的手臂。

grammar.type: 原因状语从句 + 非限定性定语从句 + when 时间状语从句
grammar.note: 主句 these noisy gasoline cars were unpopular；because they were more complicated to operate and they had difficult hand-crank starters 为原因状语从句（含并列）；which were known to break arms 为非限定性定语从句修饰 starters，when the engines backfired 为时间状语从句。

sentences.en: But in 1912 General Motors introduced the electric starter, and over the following few years steam power was gradually phased out.
sentences.zh: 但1912年通用汽车推出了电动启动器，在接下来的几年里，蒸汽动力逐渐被淘汰。

grammar.type: 并列句 + 被动语态
grammar.note: 前句 in 1912 General Motors introduced the electric starter；后句 over the following few years steam power was gradually phased out 为被动语态，phase out 表“逐步淘汰”。

sentences.en: Even as the market was declining, four brothers made one last effort to rekindle the technology.
sentences.zh: 即便在市场日渐衰落之际，四兄弟仍做出了最后一次努力，试图重新点燃这项技术。

grammar.type: as 让步/时间状语从句 + 不定式目的
grammar.note: Even as the market was declining 为 as 引导的让步/时间状语从句；主句 four brothers made one last effort；to rekindle the technology 为不定式作目的状语，rekindle 表“重新点燃”。

sentences.en: Between 1906 and 1909, while still attending high school, Abner Doble and his three brothers built their first steam car in their parents’ basement.
sentences.zh: 1906年至1909年间，阿布纳·多布尔和他的三个兄弟还在上高中时，就在父母的地下室里造出了他们的第一辆蒸汽汽车。

grammar.type: 时间状语 + while 省略状语从句
grammar.note: 主干 Abner Doble and his three brothers built their first steam car；Between 1906 and 1909 为时间状语；while still attending high school 为 while 引导的省略状语从句（=while they were still attending...）。

sentences.en: It comprised parts taken from a wrecked early steam car but reconfigured to drive an engine of their own design.
sentences.zh: 它由一辆报废的早期蒸汽汽车上拆下、但经过重新改装以驱动他们自行设计的发动机的零件组成。

grammar.type: 过去分词定语 + but 转折并列
grammar.note: 主干 It comprised parts；taken from a wrecked early steam car 与 reconfigured to drive an engine of their own design 为两个过去分词短语（由 but 连接）作后置定语修饰 parts。

sentences.en: Though it did not run well, the Doble brothers went on to build a second and third prototype in the following years.
sentences.zh: 尽管它运行得并不好，多布尔兄弟还是在随后几年里继续制造了第二辆和第三辆原型车。

grammar.type: though 让步状语从句 + go on to do
grammar.note: Though it did not run well 为让步状语从句；主句 the Doble brothers went on to build a second and third prototype，go on to do 表“接着做”。

sentences.en: Though the Doble boys’ third prototype, nicknamed the Model B, still lacked the convenience of an internal combustion engine, it drew the attention of automobile trade magazines due to its numerous improvements over previous steam cars.
sentences.zh: 尽管多布尔兄弟绰号为“B型”的第三辆原型车仍不具备内燃机的便利，但由于它相较以往蒸汽汽车有诸多改进，还是引起了汽车行业杂志的关注。

grammar.type: though 让步状语从句 + 过去分词插入定语 + 原因状语
grammar.note: Though the Doble boys’ third prototype... still lacked the convenience 为让步状语从句，nicknamed the Model B 为过去分词插入定语；主句 it drew the attention of automobile trade magazines，due to its numerous improvements 为原因状语。

sentences.en: The Model B proved to be superior to gasoline automobiles in many ways.
sentences.zh: 事实证明，B型车在许多方面都优于汽油汽车。

grammar.type: prove to be + 比较结构
grammar.note: 主干是 The Model B proved to be superior to gasoline automobiles，prove to be 表“被证明是”，be superior to 表“优于”；in many ways 为方式状语。

sentences.en: Its high-pressure steam drove the engine pistons in virtual silence, in contrast to clattering gas engines which emitted the aroma of burned hydrocarbons.
sentences.zh: 它的高压蒸汽几乎无声地驱动着发动机活塞，与那些哐当作响、散发出碳氢化合物燃烧气味的汽油发动机形成了鲜明对比。

grammar.type: 主谓宾 + in contrast to 状语 + 定语从句
grammar.note: 主干 Its high-pressure steam drove the engine pistons，in virtual silence 为方式状语；in contrast to clattering gas engines 为对比状语，which emitted the aroma of burned hydrocarbons 为定语从句修饰 gas engines。

sentences.en: Perhaps most impressively, the Model B was amazingly swift.
sentences.zh: 也许最令人印象深刻的是，B型车快得惊人。

grammar.type: 主系表 + 评注状语
grammar.note: 主干是 the Model B was amazingly swift；Perhaps most impressively 为评注性状语，表“也许最令人印象深刻的是”。

sentences.en: It could accelerate from zero to sixty miles per hour in just fifteen seconds, a feat described as ‘remarkable acceleration’ by Automobile magazine in 1914.
sentences.zh: 它能在短短十五秒内从零加速到每小时六十英里，1914年《汽车》杂志将这一壮举形容为“非凡的加速性能”。

grammar.type: 主谓 + 名词同位语 + 过去分词定语
grammar.note: 主干 It could accelerate from zero to sixty miles per hour in just fifteen seconds；a feat 为整句内容的同位语，described as ‘remarkable acceleration’ by Automobile magazine 为过去分词短语修饰 feat。

sentences.en: The following year Abner Doble drove the Model B from Massachusetts to Detroit in order to seek investment in his automobile design, which he used to open the General Engineering Company.
sentences.zh: 第二年，阿布纳·多布尔把B型车从马萨诸塞州开到底特律，以便为自己的汽车设计寻求投资，他用这笔投资开办了通用工程公司。

grammar.type: 不定式目的 + 非限定性定语从句
grammar.note: 主干 Abner Doble drove the Model B from Massachusetts to Detroit；in order to seek investment in his automobile design 为不定式作目的状语；which he used to open the General Engineering Company 为非限定性定语从句，which 指代 investment。

sentences.en: He and his brothers immediately began working on the Model C, which was intended to expand upon the innovations of the Model B.
sentences.zh: 他和兄弟们立即着手研制C型车，旨在进一步拓展B型车的各项创新。

grammar.type: 主谓宾 + 非限定性定语从句
grammar.note: 主干 He and his brothers immediately began working on the Model C；which was intended to expand upon the innovations of the Model B 为非限定性定语从句，expand upon 表“详述、拓展”。

sentences.en: The brothers added features such as a key-based ignition in the cabin, eliminating the need for the operator to manually ignite the boiler.
sentences.zh: 兄弟们增加了诸如车厢内钥匙点火之类的功能，从而免去了驾驶者手动点燃锅炉的需要。

grammar.type: 主谓宾 + 举例 + 现在分词结果状语
grammar.note: 主干 The brothers added features，such as a key-based ignition in the cabin 举例；eliminating the need for the operator to manually ignite the boiler 为现在分词短语作结果状语。

sentences.en: With these enhancements, the Dobles’ new car company promised a steam vehicle which would provide all of the convenience of a gasoline car, but with much greater speed, much simpler driving controls, and a virtually silent powerplant.
sentences.zh: 凭借这些改进，多布尔家的新汽车公司承诺推出一款蒸汽汽车，它将提供汽油汽车的全部便利，同时拥有快得多的速度、简单得多的驾驶操控以及几乎无声的动力装置。

grammar.type: with 状语 + 定语从句 + but 转折
grammar.note: With these enhancements 为状语；主干 the Dobles’ new car company promised a steam vehicle；which would provide all of the convenience of a gasoline car 为定语从句，but with much greater speed... 为转折补充，三个 with 结构并列。

sentences.en: By the following April, the General Engineering Company had received 5,390 deposits for Doble Detroits, which were scheduled for delivery in early 1918.
sentences.zh: 到第二年四月，通用工程公司已收到5,390份“多布尔底特律”车型的订金，这些车原定于1918年初交付。

grammar.type: 过去完成时 + 非限定性定语从句
grammar.note: 主干 the General Engineering Company had received 5,390 deposits for Doble Detroits，为过去完成时；which were scheduled for delivery in early 1918 为非限定性定语从句修饰 Doble Detroits。

sentences.en: Later that year Abner Doble delivered unhappy news to those eagerly awaiting the delivery of their modern new cars.
sentences.zh: 那年晚些时候，阿布纳·多布尔向那些急切等待交付新式新车的人们带来了不好的消息。

grammar.type: 主谓宾 + 现在分词定语
grammar.note: 主干 Abner Doble delivered unhappy news to those；eagerly awaiting the delivery of their modern new cars 为现在分词短语修饰 those。

sentences.en: Those buyers who received the handful of completed cars complained that the vehicles were sluggish and erratic, sometimes going in reverse when they should go forward.
sentences.zh: 那些拿到少数几辆成品车的买家抱怨说，这些车迟缓且反复无常，有时该前进时却在倒退。

grammar.type: 定语从句 + 宾语从句 + 现在分词状语
grammar.note: 主干 Those buyers... complained that the vehicles were sluggish and erratic；who received the handful of completed cars 为定语从句修饰 buyers；that 引导宾语从句；sometimes going in reverse when they should go forward 为现在分词作状语，含 when 时间状语从句。

sentences.en: The new engine design, though innovative, was still plagued with serious glitches.
sentences.zh: 这种新的发动机设计尽管富有创新，却仍然饱受严重故障的困扰。

grammar.type: 主系表 + though 让步插入
grammar.note: 主干 The new engine design... was still plagued with serious glitches，be plagued with 表“饱受……困扰”；though innovative 为 though 引导的让步状语（省略结构）作插入语。

sentences.en: The brothers made one final attempt to produce a viable steam automobile.
sentences.zh: 兄弟们做出了最后一次尝试，试图生产一款切实可行的蒸汽汽车。

grammar.type: 主谓宾 + 不定式定语
grammar.note: 主干 The brothers made one final attempt；to produce a viable steam automobile 为不定式作定语修饰 attempt，viable 表“可行的”。

sentences.en: In early 1924, the Doble brothers shipped a Model E to New York City to be road-tested by the Automobile Club of America.
sentences.zh: 1924年初，多布尔兄弟把一辆E型车运往纽约市，交由美国汽车俱乐部进行路测。

grammar.type: 主谓宾 + 不定式目的（被动）
grammar.note: 主干 the Doble brothers shipped a Model E to New York City；to be road-tested by the Automobile Club of America 为不定式的被动形式作目的状语。

sentences.en: After sitting overnight in freezing temperatures, the car was pushed out into the road and left to sit for over an hour in the frosty morning air.
sentences.zh: 在严寒中停放了一夜之后，这辆车被推到路上，又在寒冷的清晨空气中放置了一个多小时。

grammar.type: 过去分词状语 + 被动语态 + 并列
grammar.note: After sitting overnight in freezing temperatures 为介词加动名词的时间状语；主干 the car was pushed out into the road and left to sit for over an hour，两个被动谓语并列。

sentences.en: At the turn of the key, the boiler lit and reached its operating pressure inside of forty seconds.
sentences.zh: 一拧钥匙，锅炉便点着了，并在四十秒内达到了工作压力。

grammar.type: 介词状语 + 并列谓语
grammar.note: At the turn of the key 为时间状语；主干 the boiler lit and reached its operating pressure，两个谓语并列；inside of forty seconds 表“在四十秒内”。

sentences.en: As they drove the test vehicle further, they found that its evenly distributed weight lent it surprisingly good handling, even though it was so heavy.
sentences.zh: 随着继续驾驶这辆测试车，他们发现它均匀分布的重量赋予了它出奇好的操控性，尽管它非常重。

grammar.type: as 时间状语从句 + 宾语从句 + even though 让步从句
grammar.note: As they drove the test vehicle further 为 as 时间状语从句；主句 they found that...，that 引导宾语从句 its evenly distributed weight lent it surprisingly good handling，lend sb sth 表“赋予”；even though it was so heavy 为让步状语从句。

sentences.en: As the new Doble steamer was further developed and tested, its maximum speed was pushed to over a hundred miles per hour, and it achieved about fifteen miles per gallon of kerosene with negligible emissions.
sentences.zh: 随着这款新的多布尔蒸汽车不断被改进和测试，它的最高速度被提升到每小时一百英里以上，而且每加仑煤油可行驶约十五英里，排放几乎可以忽略不计。

grammar.type: as 状语从句 + 被动语态 + 并列句
grammar.note: As the new Doble steamer was further developed and tested 为 as 状语从句；主句为并列句 its maximum speed was pushed to over a hundred miles per hour（被动）和 it achieved about fifteen miles per gallon of kerosene，with negligible emissions 为伴随状语。

sentences.en: Sadly, the Dobles’ brilliant steam car never was a financial success.
sentences.zh: 遗憾的是，多布尔兄弟这款出色的蒸汽汽车在商业上始终未能成功。

grammar.type: 主系表 + 评注状语
grammar.note: 主干是 the Dobles’ brilliant steam car never was a financial success，never 表“从未”；Sadly 为评注性状语。

sentences.en: Priced at around $18,000 in 1924, it was popular only among the very wealthy.
sentences.zh: 1924年它定价约18,000美元，因此只在极富有的人群中受到青睐。

grammar.type: 过去分词状语 + 主系表
grammar.note: Priced at around $18,000 in 1924 为过去分词短语作状语；主干 it was popular only among the very wealthy，the very wealthy 为“the+形容词”表一类人。

sentences.en: Plus, it is said that no two Model Es were quite the same, because Abner Doble tinkered endlessly with the design.
sentences.zh: 此外，据说没有哪两辆E型车是完全一样的，因为阿布纳·多布尔对设计不停地修修补补。

grammar.type: it 形式主语 + 宾语从句 + 原因状语从句
grammar.note: 主干 it is said that...，it 为形式主语，that no two Model Es were quite the same 为主语从句；because Abner Doble tinkered endlessly with the design 为原因状语从句，no two... 表“没有两个……是相同的”。

sentences.en: By the time the company folded in 1931, fewer than fifty of the amazing Model E steam cars had been produced.
sentences.zh: 到1931年公司倒闭时，这款了不起的E型蒸汽汽车总共生产了不到五十辆。

grammar.type: by the time 时间状语从句 + 过去完成时
grammar.note: By the time the company folded in 1931 为 by the time 引导的时间状语从句，fold 表“倒闭”；主句 fewer than fifty of the amazing Model E steam cars had been produced 为过去完成时被动。

sentences.en: For his whole career, until his death in 1961, Abner Doble remained adamant that steam-powered automobiles were at least equal to gasoline cars, if not superior.
sentences.zh: 在他整个职业生涯中，直到1961年去世，阿布纳·多布尔始终坚信，蒸汽动力汽车至少与汽油汽车不相上下，甚至更胜一筹。

grammar.type: 主系表 + 同位语从句 + if not 省略结构
grammar.note: 主干 Abner Doble remained adamant that...，that 引导同位语从句解释 adamant；从句 steam-powered automobiles were at least equal to gasoline cars；if not superior 为省略结构，表“即使不是更优（至少相当）”。

sentences.en: Given the evidence, he may have been right.
sentences.zh: 鉴于种种证据，他也许是对的。

grammar.type: given 状语 + 情态推测
grammar.note: Given the evidence 为 given 引导的状语，表“考虑到”；主句 he may have been right，may have done 表对过去的推测。

sentences.en: Many of the Model E Dobles which have survived are still in good working condition, some having been driven over half a million miles with only normal maintenance.
sentences.zh: 许多幸存下来的E型多布尔汽车至今仍处于良好的运行状态，其中一些仅靠常规保养就已行驶了逾五十万英里。

grammar.type: 定语从句 + 独立主格
grammar.note: 主干 Many of the Model E Dobles... are still in good working condition；which have survived 为定语从句修饰 Dobles；some having been driven over half a million miles 为独立主格结构作补充，with only normal maintenance 为伴随状语。

sentences.en: Astonishingly, an unmodified Doble Model E runs clean enough to pass the emissions laws in California today, and they are pretty strict.
sentences.zh: 令人惊讶的是，一辆未经改装的多布尔E型车如今排放之洁净，足以通过加利福尼亚州的排放法规，而这些法规相当严格。

grammar.type: enough to 结构 + 并列句
grammar.note: 主干 an unmodified Doble Model E runs clean enough to pass the emissions laws，clean enough to do 表“足够洁净以至于能”；and they are pretty strict 为并列分句，they 指代 emissions laws；Astonishingly 为评注状语。

sentences.en: It is true that the technology poses some difficult problems, but you cannot help but wonder how efficient a steam car might be with the benefit of modern materials and computers.
sentences.zh: 诚然，这项技术带来了一些棘手的问题，但人们不禁会想：借助现代材料和计算机，蒸汽汽车会有多高效呢。

grammar.type: it 形式主语 + 转折 + cannot help but do + 宾语从句
grammar.note: 前半 It is true that the technology poses some difficult problems，it 为形式主语；but you cannot help but wonder how efficient a steam car might be，cannot help but do 表“不禁做”，how... 为宾语从句；with the benefit of modern materials and computers 为状语。

sentences.en: Under the current pressure to improve automotive performance and reduce emissions, it is not unthinkable that the steam car may rise again.
sentences.zh: 在当前提升汽车性能、减少排放的压力之下，蒸汽汽车东山再起并非不可想象。

grammar.type: 不定式定语 + it 形式主语 + 主语从句
grammar.note: Under the current pressure to improve automotive performance and reduce emissions 为状语，to improve... 为不定式作定语修饰 pressure；主句 it is not unthinkable that...，it 为形式主语，that the steam car may rise again 为主语从句。

questions.title: Questions 14–20
instructions: Reading Passage 2 has seven paragraphs, A–G.
Choose the correct heading for each paragraph from the list of headings below.
Write the correct number, i–viii, in boxes 14–20 on your answer sheet.
i A period in cold conditions before the technology is assessed
ii Marketing issues lead to failure
iii Good and bad aspects of steam technology are passed on
iv A possible solution to the issues of today
v Further improvements lead to commercial orders
vi Positive publicity at last for this quiet, clean, fast vehicle
vii A disappointing outcome for customers
viii A better option than the steam car arises

questions.title: Questions 21–23
instructions: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
Write the correct letter in boxes 21–23 on your answer sheet.

items.prompt: What point does the writer make about the steam car in Paragraph B? A Its success was short-lived. B Not enough cars were made. C Car companies found them hard to sell. D People found them hard to drive.

When building their first steam car, the Doble brothers A constructed all the parts themselves. B made written notes at each stage of the construction. C needed several attempts to achieve a competitive model. D sought the advice of experienced people in the car industry.
In order to produce the Model C, the Doble brothers A moved production to a different city. B raised financial capital. C employed an additional worker. D abandoned their earlier designs.

questions.title: Questions 24–26
instructions: Choose ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER from the passage for each answer.
Write your answers in boxes 24–26 on your answer sheet.

items.prompt: A later version of the Model E raised its 24 ____ , while keeping its emissions extremely low.
Under 25 ____ cars were produced before the company went out of business.
They are straightforward to maintain, and they satisfy California’s 26 ____ emissions laws.
